title: Diagnose an Event Listener Memory Leak
description: A notification service leaks memory because event listeners are registered inside a batch-processing loop but never deregistered, accumulating hundreds of thousands of handler closures.
alert:
notification-svc OOMKilled (2 occurrences, last 30 min)
Container memory grew from 150MB to 2.1GB over 25 minutes.
Severity: CRITICAL
initial_log_excerpt:
[notification-svc] INFO service started
[notification-svc] INFO registering event listener for user_signup
[notification-svc] INFO event processed: user_signup, batch=1000
[event-bus] WARN high listener count on topic user_signup

notification-svc: [notification-svc] INFO registering event listener for user_signup
[notification-svc] INFO registering event listener for user_signup
[notification-svc] WARN memory pressure detected, GC triggered
event-bus: [event-bus] WARN high listener count on topic user_signup
[event-bus] INFO other topics listener counts normal: order_placed=1, payment_complete=1
user-service: [user-service] INFO user_signup event published to event-bus

deployment_history.summary: notification-svc: add user_signup event handler for welcome emails
event-bus: increase max partition count to 64

diffs.deploy-187:
diff --git a/notification-svc/handlers.py b/notification-svc/handlers.py
+def handle_batch(events):
+    for event in events:
+        bus.register_listener('user_signup', on_signup)
+        send_welcome_email(event.user_id)
+# BUG: listener is registered inside the loop but never removed

2026-04-06T09:25:00Z.note: EventHandler instances are accumulating — each batch registers new listeners without removing old ones. Linear growth correlates with listener_count metric.

accepted_remediations: register listener once outside the loop
move register_listener out of handle_batch loop
